verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the beta subunit of the mitochondrial F1-ATP synthase, primarily resident on the inner mitochondrial membrane matrix face. However, there is well-documented evidence that ATP synthase subunits, including the beta subunit, are expressed on the plasma membrane surface of various cell types (tumor cells, endothelial cells, hepatocytes), where they function as cell-surface receptors for angiostatin, HDL, and other ligands. This ectopic surface pool is not GPI-anchored or classically trafficked but represents a documented minority PM fraction, qualifying as dual_localization / contextual surface biology.
